meta.title: Production Deployment Guide
meta.excerpt: End-to-end deployment guide covering Docker, PaaS, Strapi Cloud, Nginx reverse proxy, backup strategies, performance tuning, and monitoring setup.

props.body: PaaS vs Docker/VPS vs Strapi Cloud comparison

props.text: Choose the deployment strategy that matches your team's expertise, budget, and scalability needs.
**Best for**: Teams wanting zero DevOps overhead
**Providers**: Vercel, Netlify, Railway, Render
**Pros**: - Zero infrastructure management - Automatic scaling - Built-in CI/CD - Global CDN included - SSL certificates automated
**Cons**: - Less control over infrastructure - Vendor lock-in - Higher cost at scale - Limited customization
**Cost**: $20-200/month depending on traffic
**Best for**: Teams needing full control and cost optimization
**Providers**: DigitalOcean, Linode, Hetzner, AWS EC2
**Pros**: - Complete infrastructure control - Predictable fixed costs - Custom performance tuning - No vendor lock-in
**Cons**: - Requires DevOps expertise - Manual scaling - Security maintenance burden - Self-managed backups
**Cost**: $10-100/month for VPS + time investment
**Best for**: Strapi-specific hosting with managed CMS
**Pros**: - Optimized for Strapi performance - Managed database and media - Built-in CDN - Automatic updates
**Cons**: - More expensive than DIY - Strapi-only (not for Next.js)
**Cost**: $15-99/month per project

props.code:
Next.js Frontend → Vercel (PaaS)
Strapi CMS → Railway/Render (PaaS) or VPS
Database → Managed Postgres (Supabase, Neon, Railway)
Media → S3/Cloudflare R2
Cache → Upstash Redis

props.text: This hybrid approach balances ease-of-use with cost optimization.

props.body: Dockerfile, Docker Compose, and Nginx configuration

props.text: Full Docker setup for production deployment on a VPS.

props.code:
# Dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS base

# Dependencies
FROM base AS deps
RUN apk add --no-cache libc6-compat
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json pnpm-lock.yaml ./
RUN corepack enable pnpm && pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

# Builder
FROM base AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN corepack enable pnpm && pnpm build

# Runner
FROM base AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1

RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs
RUN adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs

COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static

USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000
ENV PORT=3000

CMD ["node", "server.js"]

# docker-compose.yml
version: '3.8'

services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: production_db
      POSTGRES_USER: ${DB_USER}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD}
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${DB_USER}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/data
    command: redis-server --appendonly yes

  strapi:
    image: strapi/strapi:latest
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      DATABASE_CLIENT: postgres
      DATABASE_HOST: postgres
      DATABASE_NAME: production_db
      DATABASE_USERNAME: ${DB_USER}
      DATABASE_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD}
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET}
      ADMIN_JWT_SECRET: ${ADMIN_JWT_SECRET}
      APP_KEYS: ${APP_KEYS}
    volumes:
      - strapi_uploads:/srv/app/public/uploads
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

  nextjs:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://${DB_USER}:${DB_PASSWORD}@postgres:5432/production_db
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      NEXT_PUBLIC_STRAPI_URL: ${STRAPI_URL}
      STRAPI_API_TOKEN: ${STRAPI_API_TOKEN}
    depends_on:
      - postgres
      - redis
      - strapi

  nginx:
    image: nginx:alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
    volumes:
      - ./nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro
      - ./ssl:/etc/nginx/ssl:ro
    depends_on:
      - nextjs
      - strapi

volumes:
  postgres_data:
  redis_data:
  strapi_uploads:

# Initial deployment
docker-compose up -d --build

# View logs
docker-compose logs -f

# Update deployment
docker-compose pull
docker-compose up -d --build

# Stop services
docker-compose down

props.body: Automated scripts for database + uploads backup and restore

props.text: Automated backup strategy with retention policies and disaster recovery procedures.

props.code:
#!/bin/bash
# backup.sh

BACKUP_DIR="/var/backups/app"
DATE=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
RETENTION_DAYS=7

mkdir -p $BACKUP_DIR

# Backup PostgreSQL
echo "Backing up database..."
docker exec postgres pg_dump -U $DB_USER production_db | \
  gzip > $BACKUP_DIR/db_$DATE.sql.gz

# Backup Strapi uploads
echo "Backing up uploads..."
docker run --rm \
  -v strapi_uploads:/data \
  -v $BACKUP_DIR:/backup \
  alpine tar czf /backup/uploads_$DATE.tar.gz -C /data .

# Backup Redis (if persistent data)
echo "Backing up Redis..."
docker exec redis redis-cli BGSAVE
sleep 5
docker cp redis:/data/dump.rdb $BACKUP_DIR/redis_$DATE.rdb

# Delete old backups
echo "Cleaning old backups..."
find $BACKUP_DIR -name "*.gz" -mtime +$RETENTION_DAYS -delete
find $BACKUP_DIR -name "*.rdb" -mtime +$RETENTION_DAYS -delete

echo "Backup complete: $DATE"

#!/bin/bash
# restore.sh

if [ -z "$1" ]; then
  echo "Usage: ./restore.sh YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS"
  exit 1
fi

BACKUP_DIR="/var/backups/app"
DATE=$1

# Restore database
echo "Restoring database..."
gunzip < $BACKUP_DIR/db_$DATE.sql.gz | \
  docker exec -i postgres psql -U $DB_USER production_db

# Restore uploads
echo "Restoring uploads..."
docker run --rm \
  -v strapi_uploads:/data \
  -v $BACKUP_DIR:/backup \
  alpine tar xzf /backup/uploads_$DATE.tar.gz -C /data

# Restore Redis
echo "Restoring Redis..."
docker stop redis
docker cp $BACKUP_DIR/redis_$DATE.rdb redis:/data/dump.rdb
docker start redis

echo "Restore complete"

# Add to crontab
crontab -e

# Daily backup at 2 AM
0 2 * * * /opt/app/backup.sh >> /var/log/backup.log 2>&1

# Weekly backup to S3 (Sunday 3 AM)
0 3 * * 0 aws s3 sync /var/backups/app s3://my-backups/app/

props.text: 1. ✅ Restore database from most recent backup 2. ✅ Restore media uploads 3. ✅ Verify environment variables 4. ✅ Restart all services 5. ✅ Run health checks 6. ✅ Test critical user flows 7. ✅ Monitor error rates for 24h 8. ✅ Document incident for post-mortem

props.body: Database optimization, Redis caching, CDN setup

props.text: Performance optimization strategies for production loads.

props.code:
# postgresql.conf (for 4GB RAM server)
shared_buffers = 1GB
effective_cache_size = 3GB
maintenance_work_mem = 256MB
max_connections = 100
work_mem = 10MB

props.body: Health checks, APM tools, alert thresholds

props.text: Production monitoring and alerting setup.

props.code:
# Monitor health endpoint every 5 minutes
*/5 * * * * curl -f https://yourdomain.com/api/health || \
  echo "Health check failed" | mail -s "Site Down" admin@yourdomain.com

# alerting.yml (for monitoring service)
alerts:
  - name: High Error Rate
    condition: error_rate > 5%
    window: 5m
    notification: email, slack
    
  - name: Slow Response Time
    condition: p95_latency > 2000ms
    window: 10m
    notification: slack
    
  - name: Database Connection Pool Exhausted
    condition: db_connections > 90%
    window: 1m
    notification: email, pagerduty
    
  - name: High Memory Usage
    condition: memory_usage > 85%
    window: 5m
    notification: slack
